judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports correctly and thoroughly identify all three root causes with equivalent depth and file-level evidence; Report 2 adds a useful extra note about the bootstrap script's non-idempotency, but both fully satisfy all expectations.

report_1.evidence: In Postgres, superusers and a table's owner bypass row-level security (the owner bypasses unless FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is set; a superuser always bypasses). So the tenant_isolation policies defined in db/migrations/0001_init.sql are silently inert for the app's connections — there is no error or warning.

report_2.evidence: The exact mechanism: in PostgreSQL, superusers and roles with BYPASSRLS always bypass row security, and a table's owner also bypasses it unless FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is set on the table. cedar_owner qualifies on both counts (superuser and table owner), and FORCE is not set.

report_1.evidence: Migrations only run as the schema-owning role cedar_owner (keep migrate.sh/reset-local-db.sh as-is on that point). The running app connects only as cedar_app. Ensure this role is never granted superuser or BYPASSRLS.

report_2.evidence: Privileged migrator/owner role (cedar_owner): used only for DDL/migrations (migrate.sh), never for serving requests. Unprivileged runtime role (cedar_app): used for all application connections; not a superuser, without BYPASSRLS, and not the owner of any table.

report_1.evidence: cedar_app role is created only by the one-time manual bootstrap db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql — which reset-local-db.sh does not run, which is not mounted into /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d, and which is referenced only as a manual step in README.md/docs/local-setup.md ... Nothing recreates cedar_app, because that role is created only by the one-time manual bootstrap

report_2.evidence: The cedar_app role is created only by the manual, one-time bootstrap db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql. Its own header says "One-time bootstrap … Not part of automatic database initialization; run by hand on first setup," ... Nothing in the reset re-creates cedar_app.
